summary.intent: Build a browser viewing feature for the Moltis agent platform, allowing users to see and interact with browser sessions (both agent-created and manually created) through a UI panel.
summary.outcome: Delivered a fully functional browser viewing panel with session creation, live screencast streaming, click/scroll interaction, URL autocomplete, and cross-session state management. Fixed multiple bugs along the way including serde null handling, shared manager instances, WebSocket subscription missing event, screencast relay race condition, and CDP coordinate mapping.

repo: BrowserTool and RealBrowserService were separate instances with separate BrowserManagers — sessions created by the tool were invisible to the UI service until shared via Arc<OnceCell>

The v4 WebSocket protocol requires clients to explicitly subscribe to event types via a subscription list in websocket.js; events not listed in Object.keys(eventHandlers) are silently dropped by broadcast.rs
Screencast relay exits when tx.send() finds 0 receivers — CDP sends the first frame before the UI subscriber connects, causing a race condition
CDP requires both deltaX and deltaY to be present (not conditional) for mouseWheel events — omitting zero values causes a CDP error
Screencast metadata includes offset_top and device dimensions (not raw viewport) — coordinate mapping must use these values, not the canvas CSS size
about:blank is rejected by validate_url() which only allows http/https schemes
serde #[serde(default)] handles missing keys but not explicit null values — LLMs (MiniMax) send null for all optional fields causing u64 deserialization failures
The workspace denies .expect() calls even in test code (clippy deny)

code.finding: Added null-stripping via obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) before deserialization in execute()
Changed relay exit on SendError to continue (drop frame) so relay survives until subscribers arrive
Subscription filter: clients must be subscribed to an event type or subscriptions must be None (wildcard) for broadcast to reach them
browser.screencast.frame was missing from the event subscription list — added to fix frames never reaching the UI
Canvas event listeners were attached in useEffect([]) which ran before canvas was conditionally rendered — fixed with ref callback (canvasRefCallback)
Added integration tests for screencast, click, and scroll; parallel tests conflict over browser profiles — requires --test-threads=1 or ephemeral profiles
CDP dispatchMouseEvent requires deltaX and deltaY both present for mouseWheel type, not conditionally

workflow: Diagnostic logging at every stage of a relay chain (CDP → registry → subscriber → WS → client) is essential for tracing async frame delivery bugs
Integration tests with real browsers must use ephemeral profiles to avoid lock conflicts when run in parallel
YubiKey-signed git pushes require manual tap during automated push sequences
Biome linting should be run before every JS commit to maintain consistency

friction: Multiple YubiKey tap interruptions during automated push sequences required manual intervention
The 'Waiting for first frame' bug required 4 separate investigation rounds before root cause (missing WebSocket subscription) was identified
Coordinate mapping was wrong in multiple ways simultaneously: border offsets, wrong aspect ratio (16/10 vs 16/9), missing offset_top from metadata — hard to isolate without e2e tests
Scroll events were silently failing at the CDP layer (both deltaX and deltaY required) with no error surfaced to the UI
Parallel browser integration tests fight over shared browser profiles causing lock errors
The CUDA clippy error in moltis-voice creates noise when running workspace-wide clippy checks
open_items: Per-agent browser profiles for session isolation — currently single global profile in host mode
Google Suggestions autocomplete in the URL bar requires CORS-friendly endpoint — suggestqueries.google.com with client=firefox was planned but may need server-side proxying
Playwright e2e tests written but interaction tests (click accuracy, scroll) need real CDP connection to verify coordinate mapping end-to-end
Screencast frame delivery in sandbox mode vs host mode parity not fully verified — integration test only confirmed host mode
Session cookie export feature was removed from the UI but backend still supports it — may want to re-expose
Mousemove throttling (16ms) and wheel batching (50ms) may need tuning for responsiveness vs server load tradeoff
